title: OnOptions
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: AI Can Talk You Out of Being Right. New Research Shows How
items.description: When you challenge generative AI, it may not just correct itself but persuade you to override your expertise.

items.title: OpenAI’s Leadership Drama Reveals 5 Hard Truths About Running a High-Growth Company
items.description: When innovation moves at warp speed, leadership gets stress-tested in ways most companies never experience.

items.title: Reagan Economist Sounds the Alarm: AI Is ‘Overhyped and Potentially Dangerous’
items.description: Market expert Steve Hanke predicts AI bubble will burst if Big Tech fails to meet its “spectacular” revenue forecasts.

items.title: ‘Zizian’ cult member marked by beliefs about veganism, animal rights, gender identity and AI bailed out of jail
items.description: Police in Maryland connected Jack “Ziz” LaSota, Michelle Zajko and Daniel Blank to homicide investigations, according to court documents.

items.title: 3 Ways to Build Rapport and Understanding Before You Negotiate
items.description: When you learn how to connect with people, you will be able to better understand, collaborate, and negotiate with them.

items.title: After United flights roll off tarmac, lose tires, audit finds FAA isn’t staffed enough to do regular inspections
items.description: “United has long advocated in favor of providing the FAA with the resources it needs for its important work," the carrier said.

items.title: Utah mother who self-published a children’s book about husband’s death now on trial for his murder
items.description: Kouri Richins, 35, faces nearly counts including aggravated murder, attempted murder, forgery, mortgage fraud and insurance fraud.

items.title: PacifiCorp settles wildfire claims for over half a billion dollars
items.description: The settlement resolves claims that electrical lines negligently started fires in Oregon in 2020 and in California in 2020 and 2022, the DOJ said.

items.title: Why did the U.S. government sterilize thousands of Native American women in the 1970s? New Mexico is investigating
items.description: “It’s important for New Mexico to understand the atrocities that took place within the borders of our state,” said state Sen. Linda Lopez.
